UDEPT. 501: RESPONDENTS CHARLES AKHIDNEOR, FRANK PEREZ, BARBARA SMITH, SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY, LINDA MARTIN AND ANDREA AGHO'S DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION IS OVERRULED, 10 DAYS TO ANSWER. PETITION SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGES FACTS AND IS NOT UNCERTAIN. PETITIONER ALLEGES THAT HE IS DISABLED AND SUCH DISABILITY HAS BEEN RECOGNIZED BY RESPONDENT SINCE 2004; THAT HIS DISABILITY REQUIRES ACCOMMODATION IN A FORM OF LIVE-IN AID AUTHORIZATION; THAT RESPONDENTS FAILED TO AFFORD HIM ALL ADMINISTRATIVE HEARINGS TO WHICH HE WAS ENTITLED; THAT THE DECISIONS RENDERED AT THE HEARINGS THAT DID TAKE PLACE WERE ERRONEOUS UNDER THE STATUTES AND REGULATIONS; AND THAT THE RESPONDENTS FAILED TO PROVIDE HIM WITH ALL THE NOTICES TO WHICH HE WAS ENTITLED. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED. JUDGE: RONALD EVANS QUIDACHAY; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: PNOT REPORTED. (D501)

DEPT. 501: DEFENDANTS SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY, CHARLES AKHIDNEOR, FRANK PEREZ, BARBARA SMITH, LINDA MARTIN AND ANDREA AGHO'S MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFF'S 2ND AMENDED PETITION AND COMPLAINT, DISMISSAL WITH PREJUDICE AND FOR ENTRY OF JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THE COURT DECLINES TO EXERCISE ITS DISCRETION TO STRIKE AND DISMISS THE PETITION WITH PREJUDICE UNDER THE CIRCUMSTANCES IN THIS CASE. EVEN THOUGH SECOND AMENDED PETITION WAS FILED UNTIMELY, LEAVE TO AMEND IS LIBERALLY GRANTED. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED. Judge: Ronald Evans Quidachay; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: not reported. (D501)

Jon Doe, plaintiff in pro per is present.
No appearance by defendants.

RESPONDENTs CHARLES AKHIDNEOR, FRANK PEREZ, BARBARA SMITH, SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY, LINDA MARTIN and ANDREA AGHO's DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION is OVERRULED, 10 days to answer. Petitioner alleges that he is disabled and such disability has been recognized by Respondent since 2004; that his disability requires accommodation in a form of live-in aid authorization; that Respondents failed to afford him all administrative hearings to which he was entitled; that the decisions rendered at the hearings that did take place were erroneous under the statutes and regulations; and that the Respondents failed to provide him with all the notices to which he was entitled. (D501)
